item.title: neofetch
item.description: Neofetch, a command-line system information tool.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Rufus, the best way to install a clean Windows
item.description: Rufus, a great tool for creating Windows installation media, allowing you to disable Microsoft's forced AI integrations.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Trying Aurora: my first look at the future of the Linux desktop
item.description: Aurora: Image-based, atomic updates, devcontainers, homebrew, and the whole DX experience I'm testing in a VM.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Expand Aurora VM disk on Unraid and resize partitions
item.description: How to expand the virtual disk of an Aurora VM running on Unraid and resize Btrfs partitions within the operating system.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Rust eBooks Nightly — Rust books in EPUB, AZW3, MOBI, and PDF
item.description: Artur Sulej's project that automatically builds and publishes the latest versions of top Rust books as eBooks. Updated daily.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Disable git autocrlf
item.description: If you use Windows (or WSL), Git has been silently converting your line endings. One command fixes it.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Installing GitHub Skills
item.description: Comparing gh skill vs npx skills for installing and managing AI agent skills.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Velvet OS: modern kernels for old ARM hardware
item.description: Mental note about Velvet OS, an interesting project to update kernels on old ARM devices.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Video Creation with Gemini: Seville Characters
item.description: Testing Gemini's video generation with Seville-based characters.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Google Stitch: UI generation and prototyping with AI
item.description: Overview of Google Stitch as a tool for generating user interfaces and interactive prototypes using artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: rbw sync: missing field `access_token`
item.description: Known rbw bug when syncing with Bitwarden or Vaultwarden: "failed to parse JSON: missing field access_token". The fix is to clear the local cache.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: rbw-env: Export environment variables from Bitwarden
item.description: Using rbw to export environment variables from Bitwarden, useful for setting API keys securely without storing them on disk.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Crit: The Review Loop for Agents
item.description: Crit is a tool designed to close the code review loop for AI-generated code, providing an intuitive and efficient web interface.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Hidden gems in pi-powerline-footer
item.description: Two features of the pi-powerline-footer plugin that go beyond the typical footer with model information.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Agent Browser: Browser Navigation for AI Agents
item.description: agent-browser is a tool from Vercel that allows AI agents to navigate the web and perform actions just like a human would, easily integrating with agent frameworks.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Tailscale Aperture: AI Gateway Without Distributing API Keys
item.description: Tailscale's Aperture centralizes LLM access by eliminating the need to distribute API keys to developers, using Tailscale identity for authentication.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: sem: Semantic Version Control
item.description: Discovering sem, a CLI tool that understands your code's semantics and tells you what entities changed, not just what lines.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: os.click: Operating System ISO Download Center
item.description: Quick reminder about os.click for downloading official ISOs.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: From net-tools to iproute2: command equivalents
item.description: A guide to equivalents between classic net-tools commands (ifconfig, route, arp) and modern iproute2 commands (ip).
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Fixing Rodney Chrome Error on Wayland
item.description: How to fix Rodney crash when starting Chrome on Wayland systems by using the system Chrome.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Record and Share your Terminal Sessions with asciinema
item.description: Discover asciinema, the perfect tool to record your terminal sessions in a lightweight, reproducible, and easy-to-share way.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: kitty-rbw: Fast Bitwarden access from your kitty terminal
item.description: Introducing kitty-rbw, a "kitten" for the kitty terminal that allows you to search and use rbw (Bitwarden) credentials instantly using fzf.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: rbw: The Bitwarden terminal client you should be using
item.description: How to install and configure rbw, a Rust implementation of the Bitwarden client, and how to integrate it into your workflows with pi-agent.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: DeepDiff: The Swiss Army Knife for Data Comparison in Python
item.description: When working with structured data in Python, we often need to compare two dictionaries, JSONs, or complex objects to find out what has changed. While direct comparison (==) is useful, sometimes we need to understand exactly what is different: which keys were added, which were removed, or where values have changed …
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Rendering Markdown in the terminal with Glow
item.description: Discover Glow, a tool to render Markdown directly in the terminal, ideal for reading documentation and READMEs.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Shot-scraper: Automated Web Screenshots
item.description:
Have you ever needed to take screenshots of a web page programmatically? Perhaps to document your project, monitor UI changes, or generate images for social media. There are many ways to do it, but shot-scraper stands out for its ease of use and power.

Created by Simon Willison, shot-scraper is …
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Stern: Kubernetes Logs on Steroids
item.description:
If you work with Kubernetes, you've probably suffered through the kubectl logs command. It's useful for viewing the logs of a specific pod, but it falls short when you have multiple replicas of a service or when pods restart and change names.

This is where Stern comes in.

Stern allows …
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Managing GitHub installations with gah
item.description: Install binaries directly from GitHub releases easily with gah.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: broot, a different way to navigate your directories
item.description: broot is a terminal tool for interactive directory exploration with fuzzy search, multiple panels, and file preview.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Multiline YAML: Understanding and testing complex strings
item.description: Explore YAML options for effectively handling multiline strings, with an interactive demo that allows you to test them in real-time.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Pandas Styler: Improve the Presentation of your DataFrames
item.description: Discover how the Pandas Styler object allows you to apply conditional formatting and CSS styles to your DataFrames for better data visualization and presentation.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: `fresh`: My preferred terminal editor for daily use
item.description: Discover fresh, a fast, modern, modeless terminal text editor, ideal for casual file editing, surpassing nano and vim in simplicity for regular use.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: How to View Files in an RPM Package on Linux
item.description: Quick guide to inspecting the contents of RPM packages and determining which packages own specific files on your Linux system.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Kooha: Simple and Elegant Screen Recording on Linux
item.description: Discover Kooha, a minimalist screen recording application for Linux that combines ease of use with essential features.

item.title: OpenEvidence: The Leading Medical Information Platform for Professionals
item.description: Exploring OpenEvidence, a leading platform that consolidates medical information, clinical findings, and practice guidelines for healthcare professionals in the US.

item.title: `direnv`: Manage your development environments intelligently
item.description: Discover direnv, a shell extension that automatically loads and unloads environment variables when changing directories, keeping your configuration clean and organized.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: `docker-http-https-echo`: An Essential Tool for Network Debugging
item.description: mendhak/docker-http-https-echo is a simple Docker container that facilitates debugging network configurations, proxies, and load balancers.

item.title: Kubegres: Simplified PostgreSQL Management on Kubernetes
item.description: Discover Kubegres, a Kubernetes operator that simplifies the deployment and management of highly available and scalable PostgreSQL clusters.

item.title: TCP Connection States in GNU/Linux: netstat and ss
item.description: Understand the different TCP connection states reported by netstat and ss, from establishment to termination, for better network diagnosis.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Reduce Noise in Your Python Logs: A Smart Approach
item.description: Learn how to configure Python's logging module to avoid message overload from third-party libraries, keeping your logs clean and useful.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Kitty as a Quick Access Terminal: The Convenience of a Dropdown
item.description: Configure Kitty for instant and efficient access, combining the power of a modern terminal with the convenience of a dropdown.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Essential Keyboard Shortcuts in Kitty: Mastering Your Terminal
item.description: A quick guide to the most useful Kitty keyboard shortcuts to optimize your command-line workflow.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: From Yakuake to Kitty: My Transition to a Modern Terminal
item.description: Exploring the reasons and benefits of switching from Yakuake to Kitty for a more powerful and customizable terminal experience.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Poe the Poet: Automating Tasks in Python Projects with uv
item.description: Discover how Poe the Poet allows you to define and execute custom commands directly from your pyproject.toml, optimizing your workflow in uv environments.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: How I Use Git Worktrees for Concurrent Work
item.description: A practical approach to using git worktrees and maximizing productivity with multiple simultaneous work contexts
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Hybrid Search with SQLite: Vector + Full-Text
item.description: Combining vector search and full-text search in SQLite using Reciprocal Rank Fusion for better results
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Geospatial Data Conflation with DuckDB and Embeddings
item.description: Advanced techniques to integrate geospatial data sources using DuckDB, H3, Ollama, and embedding models
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: HTTPX: Modern HTTP Client for Python
item.description: HTTPX emerges as the natural successor to requests, offering async support, HTTP/2, and a modern API for contemporary Python applications.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Server-Sent Events: Simplified Real-Time Communication
item.description: Server-Sent Events offer a simple and efficient alternative to WebSockets for unidirectional server-to-client communication in web applications
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: MobaXterm. Much more than an ssh client

item.title: PosteRazor: Create Giant Posters from Any Image
item.description: PosteRazor converts any image into a giant poster by splitting the image into printable pages that you can assemble
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Unread Mail in Gmail's Primary Tab
item.description:
As always, another reminder note.

If you filter mail in gmail by unread (is:unread), you get all unread mail, which in my case always includes hundreds of notifications, social, promotions, or forums which is usually not what I'm looking for.

To search for unread emails from the Primary tab …
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Technical Documentation with Sphinx, Paver, and Cog
item.description: Automated workflow to create technical documentation using Sphinx, Paver, and Cog, eliminating repetitive tasks and manual errors in code examples
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Determining RAM slots in use on Linux
item.description: Linux commands to determine how many RAM slots are occupied and their capacity using dmidecode, lshw, and hardware inspection techniques
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Introduction to High-Frequency Trading Systems
item.description: Fundamental concepts about High-Frequency Trading (HFT) systems, programs that execute thousands of trades per day in fractions of a second
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: How to Find Live Hosts on Your Local Network
item.description: Methods and tools to discover active devices on local networks using nmap, arp-scan, netdiscover, and other reconnaissance techniques
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Powerful Command Line Tools for Developers
item.description: Collection of essential command line tools for developers: curl, ngrep, netcat, sshuttle, siege, and mitmproxy
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Sysdig: Cloud-Native Security Platform with AI
item.description: Sysdig offers a comprehensive security platform for cloud-native environments that combines AI, real-time visibility, and open source innovation

item.title: Python: Create Repeating Generators
item.description: Techniques in Python to create generators that can be iterated multiple times, overcoming the "one-shot" limitation of standard generators

item.description: Stop firefox from opening the pasted url with the middle mouse button

item.title: Bootstrap: Two Links in the Same Row in Dropdown
item.description: CSS technique to place two links on the same horizontal row within a Bootstrap dropdown menu using flexbox

item.title: Galen Framework: Automated Testing for Responsive Design
item.description: Galen Framework simplifies automated testing of responsive layouts by verifying the position and appearance of elements across different devices
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: The Story Behind X-Forwarded-For and X-Real-IP
item.description: Analysis of X-Forwarded-For and X-Real-IP headers, their origins, differences, and how to determine the client's real IP in architectures with multiple proxies
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Nginx: Configuring X-Forwarded-For in Reverse Proxy
item.description: How to configure Nginx to pass the client's real IP to backend servers using the X-Forwarded-For header in reverse proxy configurations
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: SQL: Select Rows with Max Value per Group
item.description: SQL techniques to select complete rows with the maximum value of a column within groups, comparing performance of subqueries, joins, and window functions
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Repeating a function with threads
item.description: Let's use threads in python to periodically execute a function
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Install InSync on Opensuse
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: NetHogs see consumed bandwidth
item.description:
To measure the bandwidth I am using right now, I use NetHogs

By default, it looks for a network interface called "eth0". If it doesn't find it, it complains like this:

ioctl failed while establishing local IP for selected device eth0. You may specify the device on the command line …
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Google Code is Closing
item.description:
As I said, Google announced the closure of Google Code.

Today I received the email notifying me (I had a test project hosted there) that I could move my projects simply to github, the de facto repository for free software today (sorry for bitbucket and especially for mercurial).

Its interface …
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: pgcli the discovery of the day
item.description: postresql command line
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Blogging again
item.description: I am writing again.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Ajax view decorator
item.description:
To force a django view to be called only via AJAX calls.

def ajax_required(f):
    """
    AJAX request required decorator
    use it in your views:

    @ajax_required
    def my_view(request):
        ....

    """
    def wrap(request, *args, **kwargs):
        if not request.is_ajax():
            return HttpResponseBadRequest()
        return f(request, *args, **kwargs)
    wrap.__doc__ = f.__doc__
    wrap.__name__ …
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Generic function to use as upload_to
item.description:
FileFields in Django need an upload_to function that determines where the file will be uploaded. I usually have a generic function in utils.py that leaves them in a subfolder with the model name.

import os

def generic_upload_to(instance, filename):
    """
    Generic `upload_to` function for models.FileField and models.ImageField
    which …
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Django: links in object list
item.description:
In my projects I usually use links to related items in the admin change_list.

Files

  • utils.py
  • admin.py

utils.py

I have this in utils.py

from django.contrib import admin
from django.contrib.contenttypes.models import ContentType
from django.core import urlresolvers
from django.utils.datastructures import SortedDict …
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro

item.title: Settings vars processor
item.description:
I usually use this at the beginning of projects.

from django.conf import settings as django_settings
from django.core.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured


def settings(request):
    """
    Adds the settings specified in settings.TEMPLATE_VISIBLE_SETTINGS to
    the request context.
    """
    new_settings = {}
    for attr in django_settings.TEMPLATE_VISIBLE_SETTINGS:
        try:
            new_settings[attr] = getattr(django_settings, attr)
        except AttributeError …
item.dc:creator: Pablo Caro
